docket_entries.description: NTC OF LODGING ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD FILED BY PETITIONER BELL, LEONARD C.
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO PETITOINER'S WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE FILED BY RESPONDENT RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER LEONARD BELL'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS GRANTED. THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT PETITIONER SATISFIED HIS BURDEN OF PROVING HE WAS DISABLED FROM PERFORMING HIS REGULAR DUTIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Manadamus
Off Calendar Nov-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER LEONARD BELL'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS.
Appearances: Michael S. Hebel of the San Francisco Police Officers Association (861.0211) for petitioner and moving party; Nancy J. Tavernit of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3961) for respondent and opposing party.
The court made a personal disclosure, and no objections were raised. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. The weight of the evidence shows that petitioner satisfied his burden of proving he was disabled from performing his regular duties. Order signed in open court. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Manadamus
Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Continued To Nov-21-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL STEVEN HEBEL APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. NANCY J. TAVERNIT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3961 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER LEONARD BELL'S Motion For Writ Of Manadamus IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 21, 2011 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. COURTESY COPIES WERE GIVEN IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)
